records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who Kills His Son, His Father, or His Mother
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5246 - Amr ibn Shimr narrated from Jabir from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a man who kills his son or his slave.
Imam (as) said: "He is not to be killed for that, but he should be severely beaten and exiled from his hometown."
records.arabicText:
5246 - وَ رَوَى عَمْرُو بْنُ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَقْتُلُ اِبْنَهُ أَوْ عَبْدَهُ قَالَ "لاَ يُقْتَلُ بِهِ وَ لَكِنْ يُضْرَبُ ضَرْباً شَدِيداً وَ يُنْفَى مِنْ مَسْقَطِ رَأْسِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5246 - Amr ibn Shimr narrated from Jabir from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a man who kills his son or his slave.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He is not to be killed for that, but he should be severely beaten and exiled from his hometown."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who Kills His Son, His Father, or His Mother
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5247 - Ali ibn Riʾab narrated from Abu Ubaydah who said:
I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who killed his mother.
Imam (as) said: "He does not inherit from her, and he should be killed for it in a state of humiliation. Yet, I do not think that his killing would be an expiation for his sin."
records.arabicText: 5247 - رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ رِئَابٍ عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ أُمَّهُ قَالَ لَا يَرِثُهَا وَ يُقْتَلُ بِهَا وَ هُوَ صَاغِرٌ وَ لَا أَظُنُّ قَتْلَهُ بِهَا كَفَّارَةً لِذَنْبِهِ‌.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5247 - Ali ibn Riʾab narrated from Abu Ubaydah who said:
I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who killed his mother.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He does not inherit from her, and he should be killed for it in a state of humiliation. Yet, I do not think that his killing would be an expiation for his sin."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bias in Bequests Being Among the Major Sins
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5420 - Harun ibn Muslim narrated from Masadah ibn Ṣadaqah from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) from his forefathers (peace be upon them), who said that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Injustice in a will is among the major sins."
records.arabicText: 5420 - رَوَى هَارُونُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَةَ بْنِ صَدَقَةَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اَلْحَيْفُ فِي اَلْوَصِيَّةِ مِنَ اَلْكَبَائِرِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5420 - Harun ibn Muslim narrated from Masadah ibn Ṣadaqah from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) from his forefathers (peace be upon them), who said that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Injustice in a will is among the major sins."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5249 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Baṣir who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the blood money (diyyah) for a Jew, a Christian, and a Magian.
Imam (as) replied: "They are equal - eight hundred (dirhams), eight hundred (dirhams)."
I asked: "May I be your ransom, if they are caught committing an immoral act in the land of the Muslims, is the legal punishment (hadd) applied to them?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, they are judged according to the rulings of the Muslims."
records.arabicText: 5249 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ دِيَةِ اَلْيَهُودِيِّ وَ اَلنَّصْرَانِيِّ وَ اَلْمَجُوسِيِّ قَالَ "هُمْ سَوَاءٌ ثَمَانُمِائَةٍ ثَمَانُمِائَةٍ" قَالَ قُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ إِنْ أُخِذُوا فِي بَلَدِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَ هُمْ يَعْمَلُونَ اَلْفَاحِشَةَ أَ يُقَامُ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلْحَدُّ قَالَ "نَعَمْ يُحْكَمُ فِيهِمْ بِأَحْكَامِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5249 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Baṣir who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the blood money (diyyah) for a Jew, a Christian, and a Magian.
Imam (as) replied: "They are equal - eight hundred (dirhams), eight hundred (dirhams)."
I asked: "May I be your ransom, if they are caught committing an immoral act in the land of the Muslims, is the legal punishment (hadd) applied to them?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, they are judged according to the rulings of the Muslims."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5250 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Samaah ibn Mihran who reported from Abu Abdullah (as) that the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) sent Khalid ibn Al-Walid to Bahrain, where he was involved in shedding the blood of some Jews, Christians, and Magians.
Khalid wrote to the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family), saying:
"I have shed the blood of some Jews and Christians, and I paid their blood money (diyyah) as eight hundred (dirhams) each. I also shed the blood of some Magians, but you did not give me any prior instruction regarding them."
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) wrote back to him: "Their blood money is the same as that of the Jews and Christians," and he added, "They are People of the Book."
records.arabicText: 5250 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "بَعَثَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ خَالِدَ بْنَ اَلْوَلِيدِ إِلَى اَلْبَحْرَيْنِ فَأَصَابَ بِهَا دِمَاءَ قَوْمٍ مِنَ اَلْيَهُودِ وَ اَلنَّصَارَى وَ اَلْمَجُوسِ فَكَتَبَ إِلَى رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِنِّي أَصَبْتُ دِمَاءَ قَوْمٍ مِنَ اَلْيَهُودِ وَ اَلنَّصَارَى فَوَدَيْتُهُمْ ثَمَانَمِائَةٍ ثَمَانَمِائَةٍ وَ أَصَبْتُ دِمَاءَ قَوْمٍ مِنَ اَلْمَجُوسِ وَ لَمْ تَكُنْ عَهِدْتَ إِلَيَّ فِيهِمْ عَهْداً قَالَ فَكَتَبَ إِلَيْهِ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ "إِنَّ دِيَتَهُمْ مِثْلُ دِيَةِ اَلْيَهُودِ وَ اَلنَّصَارَى " وَ قَالَ "إِنَّهُمْ أَهْلُ كِتَابٍ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5250 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Samaah ibn Mihran who reported from Abu Abdullah (as) that
records.thaqalaynMatn:
the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) sent Khalid ibn Al-Walid to Bahrain, where he was involved in shedding the blood of some Jews, Christians, and Magians.
Khalid wrote to the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family), saying:
"I have shed the blood of some Jews and Christians, and I paid their blood money (diyyah) as eight hundred (dirhams) each. I also shed the blood of some Magians, but you did not give me any prior instruction regarding them."
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) wrote back to him: "Their blood money is the same as that of the Jews and Christians," and he added, "They are People of the Book."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5251 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Riʾab from Ḍurays Al-Kunasi who reported from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a Christian man who killed a Muslim. When he was captured, he accepted Islam.
Imam (as) was asked: "Should he be killed for the Muslim he murdered?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
It was then asked: "And if he does not accept Islam?"
Imam (as) said: "He will be handed over to the heirs of the murdered person. If they wish, they may kill him, if they wish, they may pardon him, and if they wish, they may enslave him. And if he possesses any tangible wealth, it will be handed over to the heirs of the murdered person along with him."
records.arabicText: 5251 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ ضُرَيْسٍ اَلْكُنَاسِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي نَصْرَانِيٍّ قَتَلَ مُسْلِماً فَلَمَّا أُخِذَ أَسْلَمَ أَقْتُلُهُ بِهِ قَالَ "نَعَمْ" قِيلَ فَإِنْ لَمْ يُسْلِمْ قَالَ "يُدْفَعُ إِلَى أَوْلِيَاءِ اَلْمَقْتُولِ فَإِنْ شَاءُوا قَتَلُوا وَ إِنْ شَاءُوا عَفَوْا وَ إِنْ شَاءُوا اِسْتَرَقُّوا وَ إِنْ كَانَ مَعَهُ مَالٌ عَيْنٌ لَهُ دُفِعَ إِلَى أَوْلِيَاءِ اَلْمَقْتُولِ هُوَ وَ مَالُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5251 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Riʾab from Ḍurays Al-Kunasi who reported from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as)
records.thaqalaynMatn:
regarding a Christian man who killed a Muslim. When he was captured, he accepted Islam.
Imam (as) was asked: "Should he be killed for the Muslim he murdered?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
It was then asked: "And if he does not accept Islam?"
Imam (as) said: "He will be handed over to the heirs of the murdered person. If they wish, they may kill him, if they wish, they may pardon him, and if they wish, they may enslave him. And if he possesses any tangible wealth, it will be handed over to the heirs of the murdered person along with him."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5252 - Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah from Abu Baṣir, who reported from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
"The blood money (diyyah) for a Jew and a Christian is four thousand (dirhams), four thousand (dirhams), and the blood money for a Magian (Zoroastrian) is eight hundred dirhams."
Imam (as) also said: "Indeed, the Magians have a book called Jamaspf."
records.arabicText: 5252 - وَ رَوَى اَلْقَاسِمُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "دِيَةُ اَلْيَهُودِيِّ وَ اَلنَّصْرَانِيِّ أَرْبَعَةُ آلاَفٍ أَرْبَعَةُ آلاَفٍ وَ دِيَةُ اَلْمَجُوسِيِّ ثَمَانُمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ" وَ قَالَ "أَمَا إِنَّ لِلْمَجُوسِ كِتَاباً يُقَالُ لَهُ جَامَاسْفُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5252 - Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah from Abu Baṣir, who reported from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The blood money (diyyah) for a Jew and a Christian is four thousand (dirhams), four thousand (dirhams), and the blood money for a Magian (Zoroastrian) is eight hundred dirhams."
Imam (as) also said: "Indeed, the Magians have a book called Jamaspf."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5253 - It has been narrated: "The blood money (diyyah) for a Jew, a Christian, and a Magian is four thousand dirhams, four thousand dirhams because they are People of the Book."
records.arabicText: 5253 - وَ قَدْ رُوِيَ: "أَنَّ دِيَةَ اَلْيَهُودِيِّ وَ اَلنَّصْرَانِيِّ وَ اَلْمَجُوسِيِّ أَرْبَعَةُ آلاَفِ دِرْهَمٍ أَرْبَعَةُ آلاَفِ دِرْهَمٍ لِأَنَّهُمْ أَهْلُ اَلْكِتَابِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5253 - It has been narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The blood money (diyyah) for a Jew, a Christian, and a Magian is four thousand dirhams, four thousand dirhams because they are People of the Book."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5254 - Abdullah ibn Al-Mughirah narrated from Mansur, from Aban ibn Taghlib, from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
"The blood money (diyyah) for a Jew, a Christian, and a Magian is equal to that of a Muslim."
records.arabicText: 5254 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ اَلْمُغِيرَةِ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ تَغْلِبَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "دِيَةُ اَلْيَهُودِيِّ وَ اَلنَّصْرَانِيِّ وَ اَلْمَجُوسِيِّ دِيَةُ اَلْمُسْلِمِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5254 - Abdullah ibn Al-Mughirah narrated from Mansur, from Aban ibn Taghlib, from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The blood money (diyyah) for a Jew, a Christian, and a Magian is equal to that of a Muslim."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5255 - Al-Husayn ibn Sa'id narrated from Fadalah, from Aban, from Zurara, from Abu Abdullah (as) who said: "Whoever was granted protection (dhimmah) by Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family), his blood money (diyyah) is complete (to that of a muslim)."
records.arabicText: 5255 - مَا رَوَاهُ اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ فَضَالَةَ عَنْ أَبَانٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "مَنْ أَعْطَاهُ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ذِمَّةً فَدِيَتُهُ كَامِلَةٌ"
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5255 - Al-Husayn ibn Sa'id narrated from Fadalah, from Aban, from Zurara, from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever was granted protection (dhimmah) by Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family), his blood money (diyyah) is complete (to that of a muslim)."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5256 - Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Abu Al-Maghra, from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"If a Muslim kills a Christian and the family of the Christian wishes to execute him, they may do so, but they must pay the difference between the two blood monies (diyyahs)."
records.arabicText: 5256 - كَمَا رَوَاهُ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْمَغْرَاءِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "إِذَا قَتَلَ اَلْمُسْلِمُ اَلنَّصْرَانِيَّ فَأَرَادَ أَهْلُ اَلنَّصْرَانِيِّ أَنْ يَقْتُلُوهُ قَتَلُوهُ وَ أَدَّوْا فَضْلَ مَا بَيْنَ اَلدِّيَتَيْنِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5257 - Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Aban, from Ismail ibn Al-Fadl, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the blood of the Magians, Jews, and Christians - whether there is any penalty upon someone who kills them if they deceive the Muslims and openly display hostility and treachery towards them.
Imam (as) replied: "No, unless he is habitual in killing them."
He further said: I asked Imam (as) about a Muslim being killed for killing a person from the People of the Book (Ahl Al-Dhimmah) or from the People of the Scripture.
Imam (as) replied: "No, unless he is accustomed to doing so and does not desist from killing them, then he will be killed and humiliated."
records.arabicText: 5257 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ أَبَانٍ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ اَلْفَضْلِ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ دِمَاءِ اَلْمَجُوسِ وَ اَلْيَهُودِ وَ اَلنَّصَارَى هَلْ عَلَى مَنْ قَتَلَهُمْ شَيْءٌ إِذَا غَشُّوا اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَ أَظْهَرُوا اَلْعَدَاوَةَ وَ اَلْغِشَّ لَهُمْ قَالَ "لاَ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ مُتَعَوِّداً لِقَتْلِهِمْ" قَالَ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمُسْلِمِ يُقْتَلُ بِأَهْلِ اَلذِّمَّةِ وَ أَهْلِ اَلْكِتَابِ إِذَا قَتَلَهُمْ قَالَ "لاَ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ مُعْتَاداً لِذَلِكَ لاَ يَدَعُ قَتْلَهُمْ فَيُقْتَلُ وَ هُوَ صَاغِرٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5257 - Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Aban, from Ismail ibn Al-Fadl, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the blood of the Magians, Jews, and Christians - whether there is any penalty upon someone who kills them if they deceive the Muslims and openly display hostility and treachery towards them.
Imam (as) replied: "No, unless he is habitual in killing them."
He further said: I asked Imam (as) about a Muslim being killed for killing a person from the People of the Book (Ahl Al-Dhimmah) or from the People of the Scripture.
Imam (as) replied: "No, unless he is accustomed to doing so and does not desist from killing them, then he will be killed and humiliated."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5258 - The Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Whoever harms one under my protection (dhimmah) has indeed harmed me."
records.arabicText: 5258 - وَ قَدْ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَنْ آذَى ذِمَّتِي فَقَدْ آذَانِي".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5258 - The Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever harms one under my protection (dhimmah) has indeed harmed me."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5259 - Buraid Al-Ijli narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he was asked about a Muslim who gouged out the eye of a Christian.
Imam (as) replied: "The blood money (diyyah) for the eye of a dhimmi (non-Muslim under Islamic protection) is four hundred dirhams."
records.arabicText: 5259 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ بُرَيْدٍ اَلْعِجْلِيِّ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ مُسْلِمٍ فَقَأَ عَيْنَ نَصْرَانِيٍّ فَقَالَ "إِنَّ دِيَةَ عَيْنِ اَلذِّمِّيِّ أَرْبَعُمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5259 - Buraid Al-Ijli narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he was asked about a Muslim who gouged out the eye of a Christian.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The blood money (diyyah) for the eye of a dhimmi (non-Muslim under Islamic protection) is four hundred dirhams."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5260 - Uthman ibn Isa narrated from Sama'ah that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"A slave is to be killed for killing a free man, but a free man is not to be killed for killing a slave. However, he must pay the slave's value and be severely beaten so that he does not repeat such an act."
records.arabicText: 5260 - وَ رَوَى عُثْمَانُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "يُقْتَلُ اَلْعَبْدُ بِالْحُرِّ وَ لاَ يُقْتَلُ اَلْحُرُّ بِالْعَبْدِ وَ لَكِنْ يُغَرَّمُ قِيمَتَهُ وَ يُضْرَبُ ضَرْباً شَدِيداً حَتَّى لاَ يَعُودَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5260 - Uthman ibn Isa narrated from Sama'ah that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A slave is to be killed for killing a free man, but a free man is not to be killed for killing a slave. However, he must pay the slave's value and be severely beaten so that he does not repeat such an act."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5261 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi that Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said regarding a man who intentionally kills his slave:
"I prefer that he frees a slave, fasts for two consecutive months, and feeds sixty needy people, and then he should repent after that."
records.arabicText:
5261 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ:
فِي رَجُلٍ يَقْتُلُ مَمْلُوكَهُ مُتَعَمِّداً قَالَ "يُعْجِبُنِي أَنْ يُعْتِقَ رَقَبَةً وَ يَصُومَ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتَابِعَيْنِ وَ يُطْعِمَ سِتِّينَ مِسْكِيناً ثُمَّ تَكُونَ اَلتَّوْبَةُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5261 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi that Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said regarding a man who intentionally kills his slave:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I prefer that he frees a slave, fasts for two consecutive months, and feeds sixty needy people, and then he should repent after that."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5262 - Humran asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who struck his slave, and the slave died as a result of the beating.
Imam (as) replied: "He must free a slave."
records.arabicText: 5262 - وَ سَأَلَ حُمْرَانُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ ضَرَبَ مَمْلُوكاً لَهُ فَمَاتَ مِنْ ضَرْبِهِ قَالَ "يُعْتِقُ رَقَبَةً".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5262 - Humran asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who struck his slave, and the slave died as a result of the beating.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He must free a slave."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5263 - Yahya ibn Abi Al-'Ala narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
"If a slave kills a free man, the family of the murdered person has the choice: if they wish, they may kill him, and if they wish, they may enslave him."
records.arabicText: 5263 - وَ رَوَى يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي اَلْعَلاَءِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "إِذَا قَتَلَ اَلْعَبْدُ اَلْحُرَّ فَلِأَهْلِ اَلْمَقْتُولِ إِنْ شَاءُوا قَتَلُوا وَ إِنْ شَاءُوا اِسْتَعْبَدُوا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5263 - Yahya ibn Abi Al-'Ala narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a slave kills a free man, the family of the murdered person has the choice: if they wish, they may kill him, and if they wish, they may enslave him."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5264 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), Commander of the Faithful, ruled regarding a mukatab (a slave who had a contract for freedom) who was killed: "The portion of him that had been freed is calculated, and the blood money for that part is paid as that of a free man, and the portion that remained in servitude is paid as the blood money of a slave."
Imam (as) also said: "A slave does not burden his family with any liability beyond himself."
records.arabicText: 5264 - : "وَ قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي مُكَاتَبٍ قُتِلَ فَقَالَ "يُحْسَبُ مَا عَتَقَ مِنْهُ فَيُؤَدَّى دِيَةُ اَلْحُرِّ وَ مَا رَقَّ دِيَةُ اَلْعَبْدِ " " وَ قَالَ "اَلْعَبْدُ لاَ يُغْرِمُ أَهْلَهُ وَرَاءَ نَفْسِهِ شَيْئاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5264 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), Commander of the Faithful, ruled regarding a mukatab (a slave who had a contract for freedom) who was killed:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The portion of him that had been freed is calculated, and the blood money for that part is paid as that of a free man, and the portion that remained in servitude is paid as the blood money of a slave."
Imam (as) also said: "A slave does not burden his family with any liability beyond himself."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5265 - Imam Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about a slave who injured a free man.
Imam (as) said: "If the free man wishes, he may take retribution (qisas) from him, and if he wishes, he may take the slave if the injury is severe enough to warrant his execution. If the injury does not warrant execution, then the slave's master must pay compensation (diyyah).
If the master refuses to pay the compensation, then the injured free man has a right over the slave equivalent to the diyyah of his injury, and the rest belongs to the master. The slave is then sold, the injured party takes his due, and the remainder is returned to the master."
records.arabicText: 5265 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: فِي عَبْدٍ جَرَحَ حُرّاً قَالَ "إِنْ شَاءَ اَلْحُرُّ اِقْتَصَّ مِنْهُ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ أَخَذَهُ إِنْ كَانَتِ اَلْجِرَاحَةُ تُحِيطُ بِرَقَبَتِهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ لاَ تُحِيطُ بِرَقَبَتِهِ اِفْتَدَاهُ مَوْلاَهُ فَإِنْ أَبَى مَوْلاَهُ أَنْ يَفْتَدِيَهُ كَانَ لِلْحُرِّ اَلْمَجْرُوحِ مِنَ اَلْعَبْدِ بِقَدْرِ دِيَةِ جِرَاحَتِهِ وَ اَلْبَاقِي لِلْمَوْلَى يُبَاعُ اَلْعَبْدُ فَيَأْخُذُ اَلْمَجْرُوحُ حَقَّهُ وَ يُرَدُّ اَلْبَاقِي عَلَى اَلْمَوْلَى ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5265 - Imam Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about a slave who injured a free man.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "If the free man wishes, he may take retribution (qisas) from him, and if he wishes, he may take the slave if the injury is severe enough to warrant his execution. If the injury does not warrant execution, then the slave's master must pay compensation (diyyah).
If the master refuses to pay the compensation, then the injured free man has a right over the slave equivalent to the diyyah of his injury, and the rest belongs to the master. The slave is then sold, the injured party takes his due, and the remainder is returned to the master."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5266 - Imam Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about a man who inflicted a mudhiha (a wound exposing the bone) on a slave.
Imam (as) said: "He is liable to pay half of one-tenth of the slave's value."
records.arabicText: 5266 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلْعَزِيزِ اَلْعَبْدِيِّ عَنْ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ شَجَّ عَبْداً مُوضِحَةً قَالَ "عَلَيْهِ نِصْفُ عُشْرِ قِيمَتِهِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5267 - Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) was asked about a slave who injured two men.
Imam (as) said: "He is to be divided between them if his crime equals his full value."
It was asked: "What if he injured one man in the morning and another in the evening?"
Imam (as) replied: "He is to be divided between them as long as the governor has not ruled regarding the first injured person. But if the governor had already ruled and handed the slave over to the first injured party due to his injury, and then the slave committed another injury afterward, then his liability for that second injury falls upon the latter victim."
records.arabicText: 5267 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي عَبْدٍ جَرَحَ رَجُلَيْنِ قَالَ "هُوَ بَيْنَهُمَا إِنْ كَانَتْ جِنَايَتُهُ تُحِيطُ بِقِيمَتِهِ" قِيلَ لَهُ فَإِنْ جَرَحَ رَجُلاً فِي أَوَّلِ اَلنَّهَارِ وَ جَرَحَ آخَرَ فِي آخِرِ اَلنَّهَارِ قَالَ "هُوَ بَيْنَهُمَا مَا لَمْ يَحْكُمِ اَلْوَالِي فِي اَلْمَجْرُوحِ اَلْأَوَّلِ فَإِنْ كَانَ اَلْوَالِي قَدْ حَكَمَ فِي اَلْمَجْرُوحِ اَلْأَوَّلِ فَدَفَعَهُ إِلَيْهِ بِجِنَايَتِهِ فَجَنَى بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ جِنَايَةً فَإِنَّ جِنَايَتَهُ عَلَى اَلْأَخِيرِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5267 - Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) was asked about a slave who injured two men.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"He is to be divided between them if his crime equals his full value."
It was asked: "What if he injured one man in the morning and another in the evening?"
Imam (as) replied: "He is to be divided between them as long as the governor has not ruled regarding the first injured person. But if the governor had already ruled and handed the slave over to the first injured party due to his injury, and then the slave committed another injury afterward, then his liability for that second injury falls upon the latter victim."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5268 - Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"If a free man kills a slave, he must pay the slave's value and be disciplined."
It was asked: "What if the value of the slave is twenty thousand?"
Imam (as) replied: "The value of a slave cannot exceed the blood money (diyyah) of a free person."
records.arabicText: 5268 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ رِئَابٍ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "إِذَا قَتَلَ اَلْحُرُّ اَلْعَبْدَ غُرِّمَ قِيمَتَهُ وَ أُدِّبَ" قِيلَ لَهُ فَإِنْ كَانَتْ قِيمَتُهُ عِشْرِينَ أَلْفاً قَالَ "لاَ يُجَاوَزُ بِقِيمَةِ عَبْدٍ عَنْ دِيَةِ حُرٍّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5268 - Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If a free man kills a slave, he must pay the slave's value and be disciplined."
It was asked: "What if the value of the slave is twenty thousand?"
Imam (as) replied: "The value of a slave cannot exceed the blood money (diyyah) of a free person."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5269 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni, Commander of the Faithful (as) said:
"The injuries of slaves are to be compensated in the same manner as the injuries of free people, according to their value."
records.arabicText: 5269 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ قَالَ قَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "جِرَاحَاتُ اَلْعَبِيدِ عَلَى نَحْوِ جِرَاحَاتِ اَلْأَحْرَارِ فِي اَلثَّمَنِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5269 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni, Commander of the Faithful (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The injuries of slaves are to be compensated in the same manner as the injuries of free people, according to their value."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Muslim Killing a Dhimmi, a Slave, a Mudabbar, or a Mukatab, or Them Killing a Muslim
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5270 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Muhammad Al-Wabishi who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a group of people who claimed that a slave had committed an offense warranting his execution, and the slave confessed to it.
Imam (as) said: "The confession of a slave is not valid against his master."
Imam (as) further said: "But if they establish evidence for what they claimed against the slave, they may take the slave for it or his master may ransom him."
records.arabicText: 5270 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْوَابِشِيِّ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ قَوْمٍ اِدَّعَوْا عَلَى عَبْدٍ جِنَايَةً تُحِيطُ بِرَقَبَتِهِ فَأَقَرَّ اَلْعَبْدُ بِهَا قَالَ "لاَ يَجُوزُ إِقْرَارُ اَلْعَبْدِ عَلَى سَيِّدِهِ" قَالَ "فَإِنْ أَقَامُوا اَلْبَيِّنَةَ عَلَى مَا اِدَّعَوْا عَلَى اَلْعَبْدِ أَخَذُوا اَلْعَبْدَ بِهَا أَوْ يَفْتَدِيَهُ مَوْلاَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5270 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Muhammad Al-Wabishi who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a group of people who claimed that a slave had committed an offense warranting his execution, and the slave confessed to it.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The confession of a slave is not valid against his master."
Imam (as) further said: "But if they establish evidence for what they claimed against the slave, they may take the slave for it or his master may ransom him."
